26 Imgur Creator Spotlight: User @AugGraves did this gorgeous watercolor!
“Do you ever wonder at what point when you gotta just say "fuck it" man?” - B-Rabbit, 8 Mile I try to keep my posts "good vibe zones". So, instead of finding the one thing in here you don't like and commenting only on that, also comment about something you DID like. Balance the negativity. Love you long time. So, I made a discord server. It's a relaxed environment where you can take/donate memes to the cause, play trivia twice a week, chat about lots of topics or about nothing at all. Very laid back/relaxed vibe. Come hang with us. https://discord.gg/AWFV7FT3RQ https://ko-fi.com/mememuseumcurator
item.dc:creator: MemeMuseumCurator

item.title: let me see those titles.
item.description:
My wife LOVES sims.
finally posting on imgur but im posting because my wife is in the hospital for kidney failure and I am not able to find a job. We are out of money and all our bills are due. House, power, water, internet, everything. We have signed up for food assistance though.
fucking tear up each and EVERY time i see any variation of this. It was a good show but i almost regret watching it. anime ptsd and sh*t.
I have less than a month left If you are willing to donate to help us here is the link https://gofund.me/4a93e0806
Cat tax I've heard so much about.
item.dc:creator: Defendant

item.title: Salted Caramel Apple Upside-Down Cake Recipe!
item.description:
https://www.triedandtruerecipe.com/salted-caramel-apple-upside-down-cake/ Salted cinnamon caramel layer: 4 tablespoons butter ½ cup light brown sugar 1 teaspoon cinnamon 2 tablespoons heavy cream ½ teaspoon flaky sea salt ½ cup pecans, lightly crushed Apples: ¾ pound Honeycrisp apples, about 2 large apples; peeled, cored, and diced 1 tablespoon brown sugar 1 tablespoon lemon juice 1 tablespoon cornstarch 1 teaspoon cinnamon Batter: ¾ cup cake flour 1 teaspoon cinnamon 1 teaspoon baking powder ¼ teaspoon nutmeg ¼ teaspoon cloves ¼ teaspoon kosher salt 4 tablespoons butter, room temperature ½ cup light brown sugar 1 egg ½ cup whole milk 1 teaspoon vanilla extract For serving: Whipped cream or vanilla ice cream, optional Make the salted cinnamon caramel: Preheat the oven to 350ºF. Lightly grease a 9-inch cake pan and line with parchment paper. Set aside. Prepare the apples: Place the diced apples in a bowl with 1 tablespoon brown sugar, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 tablespoon cornstarch, and 1 teaspoon cinnamon. Toss gently to combine. Set aside. Make the batter: Melt 4 tablespoons of butter in a small pot over medium heat. Once melted, add ½ cup brown sugar and 1 teaspoon cinnamon. Stir to combine. Cook over medium heat for 3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sugar dissolves and the mixture darkens slightly in color. Remove from the heat and add the heavy cream and flaky sea salt. Whisk until smooth. Add the crushed pecans to the caramel and stir until combined. Pour the mixture into the prepared cake pan and smooth it into an even layer. Transfer to the refrigerator for 15 minutes. Make the batter: Sift ¾ cup cake flour, 1 teaspoon cinnamon, 1 teaspoon baking powder, ¼ teaspoon nutmeg, ¼ teaspoon cloves, and ¼ teaspoon kosher salt into a small bowl. Set aside. Add 4 tablespoons butter and ½ cup light brown sugar to a large bowl. Use an electric hand mixer to cream the butter and sugar until light and fluffy. Add 1 egg and beat until smooth. Add half the sifted dry ingredients and beat with the mixer until just combined. Add half the milk and beat until incorporated. Add the remaining dry ingredients and the remaining milk, along with 1 teaspoon vanilla extract. Beat until just combined. Don’t over-mix! Assemble the cake: Remove the cake pan from the refrigerator. Pour the diced apples over the caramel and gently press them into the caramel. Pour the batter over the apples and smooth it into an even layer. Bake the upside-down cake: Transfer the cake to the oven and bake for 45 to 50 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center of the cake comes out clean. Let the cake stand at room temperature for 10 to 15 minutes. The cake should still be warm to the touch. To serve: Place a large plate over the cake pan and carefully flip the cake onto the plate. Once the cake has been transferred to the plate, carefully peel away and discard the parchment paper. Cut and serve with vanilla ice cream or whipped cream on top. Add a sprinkle of cinnamon on top, if desired. Enjoy!
The whole process!
item.dc:creator: triedandtruerecipes

item.title: Ok this made me chuckle
item.description: I’m still diving into the ICE raid on the Korean workers and the fall out from it. Side note: It’s wild that this topic is how I find out that there is a group of South Korean Charlie Kirk fans who were chanting we are Charlie Kirk. I don’t even have a meme for that one, no one does.
item.dc:creator: DownvotesStarWars

item.title: Political Dump
item.description:
I don't usually do dumps...
Hell, I rarely post anything here.
But I've seen so many of these,
From so many sites...
That I thought I'd put a few of them together in one location.
So, here I am, sharing what I've collected over the recent week or so.
Knowing you've probably seen most of these already.
But maybe some are new.
I know a lot of these help me in some discussions I have with people that don't like what I have to say.
And some are just kind of amusing.
Some just give me pause for thought.
If any of these help you in any kind of way, then great.
If they don't, you find this boring, unnecessary, or stupid, then that's kind of in you for clicking on it.
No one made you come here. You got here on your own.
You can turn back at any time, and if you choose to keep going on, that's also on you.
And if you outright disagree, that's fine too.
We can have that discussion, like mature, civilized people.
Or, you can hurl insults and name calling.
It's what the president of this country does, so I guess if it's good enough for the highest office in the land, should be good for anyone else.
But keep in mind, I do realize this is the internet, and insults are just a part of what happens here.
My feelings don't really get hurt by complete strangers who don't agree with what I have to say.
Such opinions, especially vulgar replies, usually speak more about the person posting them than they do about the person they're posting too.
But, I digress...
I hope someone feels seen in these. Heard. Understood.
I don't call for violence or revenge...
Simply understanding. Acknowledgement. To be afforded facts.
A lot of these don't provide facts and are, admittedly, propaganda. (At least in someone's mind.) I apologize for that.
But a lot of these are facts, attempting to fight against propaganda.
And if you have evidence that something isn't factual, just propaganda,
I encourage anyone to post it in the comments, along with your evidence.
Because I have learned a lot by reading comments.
Not just the idea that people are absolutely terrible (the most common lesson in any comments section),
But also, every now and again, people provide real insight to the original post (OP).
And those comments, the ones that provide something real, something sincere, something genuine, or even something educational...
Those comments are the real heroes.
But when you have leadership who push blatantly false propaganda,
When they build their cabinet with "merit", and that merit is only "loyalty" or "sycophancy",
Then it is up to the voices of the people to speak truth.
While I'm here, I don't think I have any memes addressing this in this dump, but....
The problem with all of this are the ultra wealthy. The billionaires.
They put him in office. They love him in office. I even saw a video of Bill Gates (the conservative enemy) kissing the ring of Trump.
And they have gotten exactly what they paid for.
I truly believe Trump loves America, and wants to make it the best he can...
I also believe his America and ours aren't the same thing.
His America is the America for the wealthy, the ultra rich. And that's what he is trying to help.
He never lied about this, he just let his followers believe that when he said he wants to make America great, he was referring to them.
The part that really kills me... He has actively proven he was just talking about the wealthy, the BBB being the biggest example.
And his followers still blindly believe that he's fighting for them. Thank you for your time.
item.dc:creator: twolfhound

item.title: Fascists outnumbered in Toronto
item.description: Some racist “Canada first” people tried a protest in Toronto and were MASSIVELY outnumbered by counter-protesters. Interestingly, this took place in Christie Pits Park, where major riots took place when Nazi activists targeted a Jewish-Italian baseball team, only to get their asses kicked … after which the seastika was not seen in Toronto. Fun fact: Toronto is one of the most multicultural cities on the planet, where white folks are just another minority. The city, and the surrounding region, are a major reason why MAGA-style conservatives keep losing at the national level.
item.dc:creator: Huor

item.title: As a queer person living in America all I have to say is
item.description: I fucking hate it here. I don't know if I will end up leaving, arrested, or dead. To everyone who asks why us citizens don't fight, just realize the military has complied with all unlawful orders so far and fighting against the US impossible. After all there is literally "suicide by cop" fighting against an F-35 with my mare's leg is laughable at best.
item.dc:creator: Stellaloga

item.title: Gamer down. A thank you ❤️
item.description: RIP Tony Bell, 83 years old and had clocked up a hella amount of hours on Age Of Empires II. Just wanted to cast the net and say THANK YOU to those that made this game. It brought happiness and many hours of joy to my late father-in-law. Here’s one for Tony. 95 days, 20 hours 13 weeks, 4 days, 20 hours 3 months, 5 days, 20 hours (FP ❤️ - how awesome, what a send off for an old gamer. I also now know these are rookie numbers for some of you! Ha)
item.dc:creator: SNEAKYMONK3Y
